phase-plan.purpose: Bring the repo onto the real Supabase-backed database with explicit user approval for schema changes, then establish admin access, MCP/editor tooling, and repeatable bootstrap flows.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: Postgres adapter wiring, environment setup, approved migration runs, initial admin bootstrap, customer-vs-admin access separation, customer portal route/nav boundaries, Payload MCP/editor wiring, a vendored image-generation MCP workflow with repo-local Python isolation, and the ability to seed baseline content into the real database. Out of scope: public marketing polish, CRM deal sync, and editable ops globals.

file.description: Seed workflow after migrations and admin access.
Payload MCP enablement path and local editor configuration.
Vendored image-generation MCP workflow, Python env isolation, editor wiring, and media-use policy.
Repo-local bootstrap for the vendored image-generation MCP Python environment.
Repo-local launcher used by dev tooling and MCP clients for the vendored image-generation server.
Unified dev entrypoint that starts Next dev and optional repo-owned services such as the image-generation MCP.
First-admin bootstrap path using env credentials.
Project-level Cursor MCP config that should expose Payload and image-generation MCP entries without committing secrets.
Codex example config for repo-local MCP servers.
Vendored upstream image-generation MCP server pinned in git.
Environment variable reference for admin, Supabase, and seed flows.
Deterministic Payload secret resolution used to keep builds and runtime bootable when `PAYLOAD_SECRET` is missing.
Pending migration for Payload MCP API keys and remaining quote schema deltas.
Admin-only ops dashboard route, separate from the customer dashboard.
Portal sidebar split between customer account navigation and admin tools.
Customer-only login flow and post-login routing.

task.description: Run Payload migrations only with explicit user approval and confirm the database is ready for seed content.
Create and verify the first admin account using the bootstrap script or admin login flow.
Confirm the environment is ready to seed baseline marketing content and users into the real database.

Wire Payload MCP as an env-gated part of the dev process, keep local editor configs on the same localhost origin as Next dev, and document that route availability still depends on the approved MCP API-key migration.

Vendor the image-generation MCP server, keep its Python environment self-contained with `uv`, pass root `.env` image provider settings into it, and make Cursor/Codex plus local dev use the same repo-owned startup path. The vendored submodule, repo-local `uv sync` bootstrap, Cursor/Codex examples, and env-gated dev startup are now in repo.
Keep customer login/customer dashboard separate from admin sign-in/admin ops so the public account flow never advertises Payload admin and the customer nav stays account-first.

Keep Vercel and local builds deterministic by documenting required env, pinning the Node runtime, and making Payload config resilient enough to prerender public routes when `PAYLOAD_SECRET` is accidentally missing.

questions.question: When should the approved migration/seed run happen against the current Supabase database?
Which email should be the long-term primary admin account in production?
When should the MCP API keys migration be approved so editor tooling can be enabled locally?

The default `npm run dev` path now supports image MCP as an env-gated companion service. It only starts automatically when `IMAGE_GEN_MCP_ENABLED=true`, which keeps the normal dev loop light when the image server is not needed.
Should the team eventually get a branded `/team/login` route, or is Payload admin login the long-term admin entry point?
